{{Quote|Cor'ika, we've got a hundred and fifty shabla contingency rules, everything from arresting the Chancellor if he goes gaga to reducing key allied worlds to slag if they switch sides…|RC-3222, to RC-5108/8843 and Jedi General Etain Tur-Mukan|<ref name="Order 66">''[[Order 66: A Republic Commando Novel]]''</ref>}}
'''Order 5''' was one of [[Contingency Orders for the Grand Army of the Republic: Order Initiation, Orders 1 Through 150|150 contingency orders]] that the [[Clone trooper/Legends|clone troopers]] of the [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army of the Republic]] were trained to implement for several different emergency scenarios during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]]. Order 5 dictated that if the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Republic]] [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]]—an [[Office of the Supreme Chancellor/Legends|office]] held by [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]]—was to be declared unfit to lead the Grand Army, the [[Chief of Defense Staff]] would take command of the [[Army/Legends|army]] until an alternative authority was determined.

==Description==
{{Quote|In the event of the Supreme Commander (Chancellor) being declared unfit to issue orders, as defined in Section 6 (ii), the chief of defense staff shall assume GAR command and form a strategic cell of senior officers (see page 1173, para 4) until a successor is appointed or alternative authority identified.|Order 5|<ref name="TC" />}}
Order 5 was the fifth of [[Contingency Orders for the Grand Army of the Republic: Order Initiation, Orders 1 Through 150|150 contingency orders]] created by the [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army of the Republic]] during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]]. The 150 contingency orders dealt with an assortment of emergency circumstances that the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] might confront during the war. As such, all [[Clone trooper/Legends|clone troopers]] of the Grand Army were trained to execute Order 5 without a moment's hesitation.<ref name="TC" />

Order 5 stated that if [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]]—the [[Supreme Commander (Republic)|Supreme Commander]] of the Grand Army—was deemed unfit to direct the [[Army/Legends|army]], then the Republic [[Chief of Defense Staff]] would take command of it. The Chief of Defense Staff would then form a command structure with a group of senior Republic [[Officer/Legends|officers]], and would be relieved from command once a new Supreme Chancellor was elected by the [[Galactic Senate/Legends|Galactic Senate]] or an alternative Supreme Commander was identified.<ref name="TC" />

==History==
Between [[20 BBY/Legends|20 BBY]] and [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]],<ref name="Date">The indirect mention of Order 5 in ''[[Order 66: A Republic Commando Novel]]'' takes place 94 [[Standard day/Legends|days]] before the execution of [[Order 66/Legends|Order 66]], which ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates to [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]]. The mention must have therefore occurred in either 19 [[Galactic Standard Calendar|BBY]] or [[20 BBY/Legends|20 BBY]].</ref> 995 days after the [[First Battle of Geonosis/Legends|First Battle of Geonosis]], [[Clone commando/Legends|clone commando]] [[Atin Skirata|RC-3222]] indirectly mentioned Order 5, along with the rest of the Grand Army's 150 contingency orders, to [[Jedi General/Legends|General]] [[Etain Tur-Mukan]] and squadmate [[Corr Skirata|RC-5108/8843]] during a conversation at the Republic's [[Special Operations Brigade Headquarters]] on the [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] [[Capital/Legends|capital]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]].<ref name="Order 66" />

==Behind the scenes==
Order 5 was first mentioned in ''[[Republic Commando: True Colors]]'', the third novel in the ''[[Republic Commando]]'' series, written by [[Karen Traviss]] and published in [[2007]].<ref name="TC" />

==Appearances==
*''[[Republic Commando: True Colors]]'' {{1stm}}
*''[[Order 66: A Republic Commando Novel]]'' {{Imo}}
